text.title: user-interviews Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for user-interviews as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Leading questions and pitch-mode are universal failure modes for both humans and agents under pressure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Default conversation patterns are social and confirmatory. Research requires deliberately breaking those patterns.

failure-mode.description: Recall bias: respondents reconstruct past behavior optimistically. Always ask for last specific instance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Memory is constructive and optimistic. Specificity (dates, names, sequences) anchors recall to actual events.

failure-mode.description: 5-10 interviews is enough for thematic saturation only on narrow segments. Cross-segment work needs 10-15 per segment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Homogeneous recruiting under-samples variation. Segment-specific insights require segment-specific interview volume.

failure-mode.description: Interview fatigue after 3-4 sessions in a day. Quality drops. Schedule with buffers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Interviewer fatigue reduces active listening. Listening is the methodology; fatigue breaks it.
